name: La Trattoria
description: A cozy Italian gem in a medieval Old Town alley. Handmade pasta, wood-fired pizzas, and a Tuscan wine list. Candlelit stone walls, perfect for date nights. The truffle tagliatelle is legendary.

name: Zeughauskeller
description: Iconic 15th-century armory turned beer hall. Zurcher Geschnetzeltes, pork knuckles, rosti, and excellent draft beer. Buzzing atmosphere, a must for authentic Zurich dining.

name: Hiltl
description: World's oldest vegetarian restaurant since 1898. Enormous buffet: Indian curries, Middle Eastern mezze, Asian stir-fries, Swiss classics. Rooftop terrace in summer. Great for groups.

name: Kronenhalle
description: Zurich institution since 1924 with original Picasso and Chagall paintings. Beef tartare, veal cream sauce, duck confit. Impeccable white-jacket service. Where the elite dine.

name: Cafe Sprungli
description: Iconic confectionery since 1836. Famous Luxemburgerli macarons, rich hot chocolate, light lunches, and exquisite pastries on Paradeplatz. Perfect for an afternoon break.

name: Razzia
description: Converted Art Deco cinema in Seefeld. Fresh seafood, grilled meats, seasonal salads. Soaring ceilings, moody lighting, Instagram-worthy. Great cocktails afterwards.

name: Clouds
description: 35th floor of Prime Tower, panoramic views of city, lake, and Alps. Seasonal, locally sourced menu with creative presentation. Sunset cocktails. Perfect for impressing guests.

name: Sternen Grill
description: Zurich's most famous bratwurst stand at Bellevue. Legendary veal bratwurst with crispy Burli roll since 1963. No seating, always a line, but fast. Budget-friendly institution.

name: Maison Blunt
description: Step into Marrakech in Niederdorf. Low tables, silk cushions, ornate lanterns. Rich tagines, couscous, aromatic mezze. The mint tea ceremony is a highlight.

name: Bauschanzli
description: Seasonal beer garden on a tiny island in the Limmat. Simple Swiss fare: sausages, pretzels, salads, cold beers. Magical riverside setting on summer evenings. Open May-September.

name: Helvetia
description: Stylish bistro near Stauffacher with modern Swiss classics. Venison tartare, pumpkin soup with sage butter, alpine cheese fondue with truffle. Industrial-chic, young creative crowd.

name: Lily's
description: Refined Thai in the Old Town beyond the usual pad thai. Authentic curries, som tam salads, wok dishes with weekly Bangkok imports. Only 30 seats, exclusive feel. Best green curry in Zurich.

name: Spaghetti Factory
description: Budget Italian staple in Niederdorf. Enormous pasta portions with dozens of sauces, pizzas, salads. Quirky decor, buzzy atmosphere. Popular with students and families.

name: Alpenrose
description: Traditional Swiss in a 16th-century Niederdorf building. Cheese fondue, raclette, Alplermagronen. Wood-paneled dining room like an Alpine chalet. Loved by locals, especially in winter.

name: Tibits
description: Popular vegetarian pay-by-weight buffet near the main station. Rotating salads, warm dishes, soups, desserts. Modern, no reservations. Quick, healthy lunch option.

name: Dolder Grand
description: Two Michelin stars at Dolder Grand hotel. Sweeping lake and city views. French-influenced with Wagyu, langoustine, black truffle. 4,000-label wine cellar. Special celebrations only.

name: Coco
description: Trendy Pan-Asian on Langstrasse. Bowls, bao buns, dumplings, bubble tea. Fast, affordable, perfect for quick lunch or late night. Neon interior, young crowd. Spicy dan dan noodles.

name: Nooba
description: Vietnamese and Asian fusion near Europaallee. Fresh pho, banh mi, summer rolls, rice bowls. Light healthy flavors, generous portions. Sleek design, order at counter. Near main station.

name: Rechberg 1837
description: Beautiful 19th-century Old Town townhouse. Refined Swiss-European cuisine, multi-course tasting menu with local ingredients and French technique. Garden terrace dining. Sophisticated.

name: Giesserei
description: Social enterprise restaurant in a converted Oerlikon foundry. Creative weekly-changing international menu, seasonal and sustainable. Communal tables, industrial space. Feel-good dining.
